description: Execution runner contract. Governs how WORK_PACKETs are executed against mounted repositories. Enforces scope containment, timeout discipline, and deterministic artifact collection.

execution_sequence.on_failure: Reject execution. Return schema validation errors. No mount created.

execution_sequence.action: Invoke repo_mount protocol for target_repo at target_branch.
execution_sequence.on_failure: Reject execution. Return mount validation errors. No agent dispatched.

execution_sequence.action: Route to appropriate agent based on execution_type.

routing.audit: auditor — read-only evaluation against doctrine. No worker involved.
routing.refactor: worker — structural changes within existing boundaries.
routing.build: worker — new capability construction within declared scope.
routing.migrate: db_agent + worker — schema migration with CTB validation. db_agent validates schema changes, worker executes.

execution_sequence.action: Agent operates within mounted repo scope.
constraints: No modification of doctrine, constitutional, or locked files.
No creation of files in forbidden folders (utils/, helpers/, common/, shared/, lib/, misc/).
All operations logged for audit trail.

execution_sequence.action: Artifact writer collects all outputs from execution.
outputs: Modified files as diffs against base branch.
Generated artifacts as declared in WORK_PACKET.required_artifacts.

execution_sequence.action: Auditor evaluates execution against doctrine_registry and audit_rules.
execution_sequence.on_failure: Execution log and failure reasons preserved for human review.

execution_sequence.action: If audit classification is FAIL_EXECUTION, FAIL_SCOPE, or A_MASTER, confirm ORBT telemetry artifact exists.
sequence: 1. Check for ORBT error artifact at changesets/outbox/<work_packet_id>/orbt_error_<operational_id>.json.
2. If artifact exists: validate against sys/contracts/orbt_error.schema.json.
3. If valid: record reference in execution log. Proceed.
4. If artifact missing or invalid: log warning. Proceed to certification with audit classification.
5. If severity === A_MASTER: abort certification automatically. No certification artifact produced. Record A_MASTER abort in execution log.
execution_sequence.a_master_rule: A_MASTER severity aborts certification unconditionally. No override. No manual fallback. The execution is terminal.

execution_sequence.action: Run post-audit workspace sanitation checks. Validate workspace cleanliness before certification is permitted.
sequence: 1. Execute all sanitation checks defined in sys/runtime/sanitation/sanitation_contract.json against mounted workspace.
4. If sanitation_status === FAIL: abort certification. Emit ORBT event with error_code SANITATION_FAIL. No certification artifact produced.
5. If sanitation_status === PASS: proceed to CERTIFY.
execution_sequence.on_failure: Certification blocked. sanitation_passed set to false. Execution log records specific sanitation failures.

execution_sequence.action: If ALL certification preconditions are met, generate certification artifact via signature_engine.

heir_gate.on_failure: Abort certification. Emit ORBT error with error_code CERT_HEIR_INVALID, severity A_MASTER, failure_surface certification_phase. No certification artifact produced. No auto-repair.

execution_sequence.on_pass: Certification artifact written to PR branch by artifact_writer.
execution_sequence.on_failure: FAIL certification generated with failure_reasons. Merge blocked.

timeout_policy.on_timeout: Execution terminated. Classification set to FAIL_EXECUTION with reason: TIMEOUT.

retry_policy.rationale: Each execution is a new WORK_PACKET. No implicit retries. Failures require human review and new intent declaration.

repair_policy.rationale: Inert constant. Reserved for future deterministic repair blueprint system. Does NOT alter pipeline behavior. Do NOT implement repair logic until repair blueprint contract is defined.
